title: China-to-Namibia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 61000-Series and IEC 62920 Evidence for PV Inverters in Namibia Projects
rows.target_requirement: Namibia PV inverter EMC acceptance should be built around the project specification, NSI-adopted standards where applicable, and the requirements of NamPower, the relevant RED, municipality, lender, or EPC. IEC 62920 is the dedicated EMC standard for photovoltaic power conversion equipment, while IEC 61000-series standards provide common immunity, emission, harmonic, and flicker test methods and limits. For grid-tied inverters, EMC is not only a product-label issue; it affects power quality, harmonics, protection coordination, and utility acceptance.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility test methods and limits, including harmonic current emissions and immunity requirements where applicable
NSI conformity expectations for Namibian standards adopting or referencing IEC standards
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence may be based on GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and test reports issued by Chinese laboratories. These reports may use IEC 61000-family methods, but China-side certification does not automatically prove IEC 62920 compliance for a Namibia project or satisfy a NamPower, RED, lender, or EPC documentation checklist. The relevant report should match the exact model, rating, firmware, port configuration, and operating mode supplied.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T or NB/T EMC reports are useful background evidence but should not be treated as a complete Namibia acceptance package. Exporters should obtain or map IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000-series reports, verify laboratory accreditation and model coverage, and confirm with the connecting utility or EPC whether harmonics, flicker, immunity, and conducted/radiated emissions must be retested under Namibia 50 Hz 220/380 V operating settings.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly required for grid-tied inverter acceptance, utility power-quality review, and lender/EPC technical due diligence
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or project-driven under China-side inverter certification and procurement practice; not transferable as Namibia approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on a China-only EMC certificate as Namibia project evidence. Map the file to IEC 62920 and the applicable IEC 61000-series tests, confirm model and firmware coverage, and ask NamPower, the relevant RED, municipality, EPC, or lender whether Namibia 50 Hz 220/380 V settings require additional power-quality or EMC testing.

rows.requirement: Namibia Grid-Connection Gates — NSI Conformity, ECB Licensing, NamPower/RED Connection, IEC 62116, IEC 61727, and 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied solar PV inverters for Namibia must be treated as project-specific equipment, not as automatically accepted consumer electronics. The practical approval chain is NSI conformity where Namibian standards adopt or reference IEC standards, ECB licensing or regulatory treatment for generation, supply, IPP, and net-metering projects, and connection approval by NamPower or the relevant Regional Electricity Distributor such as CENORED or Erongo RED. Namibia uses a 50 Hz low-voltage supply commonly described as 220/380 V, so inverter firmware, voltage and frequency trip thresholds, reconnection timing, anti-islanding behaviour, and utility-interface functions should be validated for Namibian settings. IEC 62116 anti-islanding and IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence are core grid-connection documents for grid-tied PV inverters.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
ECB licensing and regulatory treatment for electricity generation, supply, IPP, and net-metering projects
NamPower or relevant Regional Electricity Distributor connection approval for grid-tied systems
Namibia low-voltage grid context commonly stated as 220/380 V, 50 Hz
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408-2021, domestic anti-islanding reports, and factory grid-code settings for China. Those documents are based on Chinese regulatory acceptance and Chinese grid-connection practice. They do not create approval by NSI, ECB, NamPower, or a Namibian RED and should not be presented as a substitute for Namibia project acceptance without IEC test evidence and Namibia-specific firmware validation.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China-side GB/T and NB/T evidence does not satisfy the Namibia approval chain by itself. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding reports, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, a documented Namibia grid-code setting file for 50 Hz 220/380 V operation, and project documentation for the ECB and NamPower/RED review path. Where grid parameters differ from the Chinese factory default, the setting change should be controlled, recorded, and retested if required by the utility or project engineer.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for grid-tied PV projects: NSI conformity expectations, ECB regulatory treatment, and NamPower/RED connection approval are project gates before energisation
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China-side regulatory and grid-operator review only; it is not transferable to Namibia without project acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat NSI conformity, ECB licensing or regulatory treatment, and NamPower/RED connection approval as separate Namibia gates. Chinese GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, and GB/T 37408-2021 documents do not automatically satisfy IEC 62116, IEC 61727, or Namibia utility acceptance. Validate the inverter configuration for Namibia's 50 Hz 220/380 V network before project submission.

rows.requirement: Namibia Solar Market Context — Modified Single Buyer, IPP/Utility-Scale Projects, Rooftop Net Metering, Import Dependence, and Green Hydrogen
rows.target_requirement: Namibia has some of the world's strongest solar resources and is using solar PV to reduce heavy electricity import dependence, including imports historically sourced from South Africa's Eskom and regional power pools. Market access is project-driven: utility-scale projects, IPPs under the modified single-buyer framework, NamPower procurement, RED or municipal distribution projects, and rooftop net-metering installations can each impose different documentation routes. Green-hydrogen ambitions increase the importance of bankable IEC documentation because large renewable generation and electrolysis projects often use lender and offtaker technical specifications in addition to national approval gates.
target_standards: ECB regulation of electricity generation, transmission, distribution, supply, import, and export licensing
NamPower procurement and connection processes for national utility projects
Regional Electricity Distributor and municipal distribution connection requirements
Project-owner, lender, and offtaker specifications for IPP, utility-scale solar, and green-hydrogen-linked renewable generation
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic acceptance may include Chinese grid-code certificates, voluntary CQC or CGC inverter certification, and project-owner approvals under Chinese utility practice. These documents can help demonstrate manufacturing maturity but are not Namibia market-entry approvals. For Namibian IPP, NamPower, RED, rooftop, or hydrogen-linked projects, the acceptance package should be rebuilt around the Namibian regulator, the connecting utility, and project finance specifications.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: Namibia's solar push does not mean automatic inverter acceptance. The supplier should identify the exact route first: NamPower utility-scale procurement, IPP PPA, RED or municipal distribution connection, rooftop net metering, or hydrogen-linked renewable supply. Each route can require separate model approvals, grid studies, protection settings, commissioning tests, and documentation from IEC laboratories.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commercially mandatory: the ECB, NamPower, RED, municipality, project owner, lender, and offtaker route determines the required inverter acceptance package
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic project approvals and voluntary certifications are supporting evidence only and do not replace Namibia route-specific approvals

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Namibia's strong solar resource, import-reduction policy drivers, modified single-buyer market, rooftop net-metering activity, and green-hydrogen ambitions make solar attractive, but they do not remove the approval gates. Confirm the exact ECB, NamPower, RED, municipal, lender, and offtaker route before relying on any China-side inverter certificate.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — NSI Conformity and Namibia Project Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: For solar PV inverters supplied to Namibia, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the central international safety references for power converters used in photovoltaic power systems. Namibia project acceptance should confirm whether NSI-adopted Namibian standards, the ECB regulatory route, NamPower or RED connection conditions, EPC specifications, or lender requirements call for IEC 62109 certificates or test reports. IEC 62109-2 covers inverter-specific safety requirements and is assessed together with IEC 62109-1 general requirements.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
NSI conformity expectations where Namibian standards adopt or reference IEC standards
ECB, NamPower, RED, EPC, and lender project documentation requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, voluntary CQC or CGC certification, and factory test documentation. These can show China-side product testing but do not automatically satisfy IEC 62109-1/-2 or Namibia project acceptance. A Namibia project may require third-party IEC reports from an accredited laboratory covering the exact inverter model or model family.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China-side GB/T safety files are not a direct substitute for IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence. Exporters should obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates or type-test reports for the inverter model, confirm model-family coverage, verify that DC input voltage, AC output rating, enclosure/IP rating, firmware, and intended installation environment are covered, and align the evidence with the NSI, ECB, NamPower/RED, EPC, and lender review path.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly mandatory in practice for inverter acceptance where NSI-adopted IEC standards, utility connection conditions, EPC specifications, or lender requirements reference IEC 62109
mandatory_status.cn: China-side GB/T, NB/T, CQC, or CGC evidence supports domestic acceptance but does not by itself create Namibia safety acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A PV inverter with only China-side GB/T or NB/T safety evidence should not be treated as accepted for Namibia. Build the safety file around IEC 62109-1/-2, confirm NSI conformity expectations, and align the model coverage with the ECB, NamPower/RED, EPC, lender, and project-owner review route before shipment.
